enrichments.description: Defining approach anxiety as hesitation, overthinking, and paralysis by analysis
teaching_points: Approach anxiety stems from focusing on what could go wrong instead of what could go right
Hesitation and overthinking cause paralysis — 'paralysis by analysis'

enrichments.description: Mindset reframe: focus on positive outcomes, brain needs proof not promises
teaching_points: Recall positive past experiences with women before approaching to prime your brain
The brain needs proof not promises — push yourself to approach and prove nothing bad happens
Worst-case outcomes (rejection, being made fun of) are survivable and insignificant in hindsight

enrichments.description: Checkpoint technique: break approaching into small steps starting with intentless speaking

techniques_used.quote: hey, I really like your shirt
smiling looking a stranger in the eyes for the first time that day

techniques_discussed_negatively.quote: It's not going in direct and hey, you're fucking hot
techniques_discussed_negatively.note: Coach says NOT to use direct opener as the first checkpoint when struggling with AA

techniques_discussed_negatively.quote: Hey, let me get your number. It's none of that.
techniques_discussed_negatively.note: Coach says NOT to think about closing when you can't even make initial contact

teaching_points: Use a 'Halo checkpoint' mental model — your first checkpoint is simply speaking to a girl, not opening with intent
Hit and runs / touch and goes: compliment and keep walking, no intent to stop or escalate
Eliminate the words 'open' and 'approach' — reframe it as just speaking without intent
AA comes from thinking 15 steps ahead; focus only on the immediate next step

enrichments.description: Practical warmup: going from cold to warm to hot through intentless compliments

techniques_used.quote: hey, I really like your vest
we'll tell you you're really pretty

teaching_points: After sleeping all night or working all day, you're in a 'cold' state — use intentless speaking to warm up before real approaches
Giving compliments without intent gives positive energy that comes back to you and gets you into social mode
The progression is cold → warm (intentless speaking) → hot (ready to approach with intent)

techniques: hit_and_run: Walk-by compliment with no intent to stop or continue conversation, used as warmup to overcome approach anxiety
checkpoint_progression: Breaking the approach into sequential micro-goals (speak → stop → converse → escalate) and only focusing on the next one

checks_passed: Every technique claim has a specific segment citation and exact quote
direct_opener correctly placed in techniques_discussed_negatively per negation rule (seg 70)
No techniques tagged based on content from a different video
Evidence strength accurately reflects taught vs mention distinction
Topics match what was actually discussed — inner_game is the dominant topic throughout
No turn phases needed (talking_head, no approaches)
corrections_made: Initially considered tagging statement_of_intent as taught but coach discusses ELIMINATING intent, not using it — omitted per negation spirit
bounce mentioned at seg 62 only as an example of overthinking, not taught — omitted per mention-vs-teach threshold
remaining_concerns: Seg 135 has quality=word_mistranscription ('Tom Turow' likely 'Tom Torero', 'Egypt' likely garbled) — cited seg 138 instead for the hit-and-run example
Seg 83 references Tom Torero's hit and runs — this is attributed teaching from another coach, tagged as taught since the current coach adopts and explains it
